title: Pan-Seared Canadian Fish with Fresh Vegetable Soup
description: A hearty and flavorful soup paired with pan-seared Canadian fish. Perfect for a chilly evening.
instructions: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Season the fish fillet with salt and black pepper.
Add the fish to the skillet and sear for 3-4 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Remove the fish from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add more oil if necessary, then add the chopped carrots, celery, and onion.
Cook until the vegetables are tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Pour in the chicken broth and water, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Stir in the chopped parsley and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the soup hot, with the pan-seared fish on top.
